colon_adenocarcinoma: Microscopic image showing colon adenocarcinoma, a malignant tumor arising from glandular epithelial cells of the colon.
Colon adenocarcinoma is characterized by irregular gland formation, nuclear atypia, and invasion into surrounding tissue.
The image displays abnormal, crowded glands with loss of normal architecture, typical of malignant colon cancer.
Colon adenocarcinoma often shows desmoplastic stroma and increased mitotic activity.
This class represents a common type of colorectal cancer with aggressive growth patterns.
colon_benign_tissue: Microscopic image of normal or benign colon tissue, showing well-organized glands and uniform cell structure.
Benign colon tissue lacks features of malignancy such as nuclear atypia or tissue invasion.
The glands are regularly spaced, and the mucosa appears healthy without abnormal growths.
There is no evidence of tumor formation or architectural distortion in benign colon tissue.
This class serves as a reference for healthy or non-cancerous colon histology.
lung_adenocarcinoma: Microscopic image showing lung adenocarcinoma, a malignant tumor originating from glandular cells in the lung.
Lung adenocarcinoma is characterized by glandular or acinar structures, cellular atypia, and invasive growth.
The image reveals irregular, crowded glands and mucin production, typical of this cancer type.
Lung adenocarcinoma often presents with lepidic, acinar, or papillary growth patterns.
This class represents a common form of non-small cell lung cancer with malignant features.
lung_benign_tissue: Microscopic image of normal or benign lung tissue, showing healthy alveolar structures and uniform cells.
Benign lung tissue lacks malignant features such as cellular atypia, abnormal mitoses, or tissue invasion.
The alveoli are well-formed, and the lung parenchyma appears free of tumors or architectural distortion.
There is no evidence of cancerous growth or abnormal cell proliferation in benign lung tissue.
This class serves as a reference for healthy or non-cancerous lung histology.
lung_squamous_cell_carcinoma: Microscopic image showing lung squamous cell carcinoma, a malignant tumor arising from squamous epithelial cells in the lung.
Lung squamous cell carcinoma is characterized by keratinization, intercellular bridges, and polygonal tumor cells.
The image displays nests or sheets of atypical squamous cells with abundant eosinophilic cytoplasm.
This cancer type often shows central necrosis and may form keratin pearls.
Lung squamous cell carcinoma is a major subtype of non-small cell lung cancer with distinct histological features.
